steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $12x+108$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content:
• A) $12x+9$: forgot to multiply $9$ by $12$. Eliminated.
  • B) $12x+108$: matches the distributed expression.
  • C) $12x+21$: added $12+9$ instead of multiplying. Eliminated.
  • D) $12x+3$: subtracted instead of multiplying. Eliminated.
That matches the correct answer - B is correct.
